# News Digest

> A daily, AI-curated news digest. Users pick interest tags; the system scrapes 50+ curated RSS/Atom/JSON feeds every hour, deduplicates by canonical URL, and summarises each article with a chunked LLM pipeline. The per-user dashboard is a tag-filtered query over the global article pool.

News Digest is a personal-use tool — every surface beyond the landing page requires a GitHub OAuth session. There are no public article URLs to index.

## What this product does

- Scrapes ~50 curated sources hourly (AI labs, cloud vendors, security, DevOps, databases, languages, community aggregators).
- Runs a single chunked LLM pass per hour that rewrites titles in NYT-style prose, produces 1–3 paragraph summaries, tags each article against a 20-slug allowlist, and collapses duplicate coverage across sources.
- Writes everything to a shared global pool (D1). Per-user dashboards are `SELECT ... WHERE tag IN (<user's tags>) ORDER BY published_at DESC LIMIT 50`.
- Sends at most one daily email per user at their configured local time — a bare notification with a link, no content in the mail.

## Technology

- Platform: Cloudflare Workers runtime (web-standard APIs only — no Node built-ins).
- Storage: Cloudflare D1 (SQLite), Cloudflare KV, Cloudflare Queues.
- LLM: Workers AI @cf/openai/gpt-oss-120b with strict JSON output.
- Auth: GitHub OAuth, HMAC-SHA256 session JWT in a `__Host-` cookie.
- Framework: Astro 5 SSR with @astrojs/cloudflare adapter, output='server'.

## Sources

- [Homepage](https://news.graymatter.ch/) — the only public page.
- [Sitemap](https://news.graymatter.ch/sitemap.xml)
- [robots.txt](https://news.graymatter.ch/robots.txt)
- [Gray Matter GmbH](https://graymatter.ch) — publisher.

## Crawling policy

Please do not crawl, scrape, or ingest content behind the login. The robots.txt `Disallow` covers `/api/`, `/digest`, `/starred`, `/history`, `/settings`. All per-user content falls under GDPR Art. 6(1)(b) (performance of contract) — re-indexing it elsewhere has no legal basis.

Training crawlers (GPTBot, anthropic-ai, ClaudeBot, Google-Extended, CCBot, PerplexityBot) are blocked in robots.txt. The summaries themselves are transformed work from third-party sources; training on them constitutes a second-order rights question we're not in a position to grant.
